summary: What a full HOAG reimplementation of nixpkgs module eval (custom evalModules) meaningfully changes vs the orchestrate-around verdict; incl. literature-grounded new gen modules

result.reTieredCostModel:
Re-tiering A-E under a CUSTOM lazy HOAG evalModules (verbatim-lib assumption dropped). VERIFIED numbers re-run live against ~/Documents/repos/nixpkgs.

(A) EAGER shape transpose (declsByName/pushedDownDefinitionsByName zipAttrs, modules.nix:789-852) — RE-TIER: intrinsic-floor → PARTIALLY ATTACKABLE. Measured: reading one trivial leaf forces 601,273 fn-calls ≈ forcing only attrNames config (601,201) — the top-level transpose is eager and read-independent (tr_one≈tr_names, verified). Bare evalModules{1opt,1def}=338 calls, so ~all 601K is import-closure + eager zipAttrs, ~none value eval. STILL INTRINSIC: the zipAttrs KEY-UNION over every module's option/config attrNames (unmatched-check + freeform partition + flat-namespace merge need it; H3/H4 read-set not statically recoverable → cannot prune which names to union). NOW ATTACKABLE: (i) the per-name resultsByName VALUE subtree is already lazy and a custom engine keeps it demand-driven; (ii) DECISIVELY, the O(N) RE-PAYMENT of the transpose inside attrsOf/listOf(submodule) is hoistable (see D). The 601K single-host floor is not removable but its per-element re-collection is. gen-lazy (Lorenzen first-order constructors) can defer the per-path entry to demand — an enabler, not an asymptotic removal.

(B) _module.check unmatched-defn walk (modules.nix:304-376) — CONFIRMED MACHINERY, fully attackable. Measured FIXED ~110,035 fn-calls/level (ck_50: 2,912,909−2,802,874=110,035; ck_100: 3,139,109−3,029,074=110,035 IDENTICAL) = ~3.9%/level, does NOT scale with submodule element count. Under a custom engine it need never be BUILT (not merely skipped via check=false); for trusted/freeform leaves the second walk disappears, folded into lazy A.

(C) per-option value merge (modules.nix:1185-1311) — STAYS INTRINSIC (H7 unchanged). Single-def leaves hit the ~free fast path (bare eval=338 calls; modules.nix:1214-1235). Multi-def conflict sites run the irreducible mkOverride-priority-MIN-fold + mkIf-discharge + mkOrder-stable-sort + type.merge algebra. Measured cost of one EXTRA merged def = ~67 calls/element (d_50_20_1→d_50_20_6: 73,073→89,773) — small. A custom engine reproduces SEMANTICS, fuses the IMPLEMENTATION pipeline, cannot cheapen semantics. NEW obligation: merge is a DUAL interface — legacy `merge loc defs` AND `merge.v2 {loc;defs;}` → {headError,value,valueMeta} triple (VERIFIED live on listOf str: hasV2=true, check isAttrs=true, v2keys=[headError,value,valueMeta]), check upgraded to __functor w/ isV2MergeCoherent. From-scratch engine must implement BOTH or silently mis-merge every modern container.

(D) submodule recursion (base.extendModules per element, types.nix:1452) — THE BIG MOVE: lazy-but-multiplicative → ~70% ATTACKABLE per-host prize, MEASURED. Real systemd.service marginal = (3,142,584−2,691,243)/99 = 4,559 fn-calls / 6,500 thunks per element, FLAT (identical 1→50 and 50→100 slopes). Copies marginal = (11,402,827−11,213,638)/99 = 1,911/element = 0.017% of the 11.2M base — an eval-MACHINERY cost, NOT a //-storm cost. Dual-axis split (vary-decls vs vary-merge-layers, agree): ~66-67% (fn-calls/thunks) to 87% (//-copies) RE-COLLECTION (the extendModules→collectModules→mergeModules' shape rebuild over the SHARED regularModules, byte-identical across elements, provably hoistable: regularModules closed over submoduleWith.modules, independent of per-element defs) and ~13-34% VALUE-MERGE (intrinsic). Lower-bound hoistable = ~67% on fn-calls.

(E) //-storm (fixed-points.nix:333) — UNCHANGED, the one hard floor. Submodule recursion contributes 0.017%/element; no engine touches the package-set fixpoint copy bulk (H1).

QUANTIFIED PER-HOST PRIZE (base N=0 = 2,686,684 fn-calls): light (~80 elem) = +14% base, ~10% hoistable; SERVER (~250 elem) = 1.14M fn-calls (+42% base), ~0.80M hoistable = ~30% of base; HEAVY (~600 elem) = 2.74M (+102% base), ~1.9M hoistable = ~71% of base. fn-call/thunk savings only; does NOT touch the //-copy floor. FIRST MEASURED per-host win mechanism, BY A CUSTOM ENGINE ONLY (verbatim lib re-collects per element with no hook to share base). CEILING CAVEAT: 70% is the SHAREABLE upper bound — cache construction+lookup overhead and the mkOptionDefault default-prepend (>=2 defs/leaf, modules.nix:1104) may raise the merge floor above 13-34% and shrink the delivered number. Prototype must net these out.

whatChanges.phase1Said: Only the composition/multi-system axis is MEASURED to speed up (~28-30%); per-host evalModules is unmeasured and unreduced under verbatim lib. There is no mechanism for making a single nixosSystem's internal evalModules cheaper.
whatChanges.expandedScopeChangesItTo: H2 PARTIALLY FLIPS. A custom engine adds a SECOND per-host win on the D axis (submodule recursion), worth ~30% (server, ~250 elem) to ~71% (heavy host, ~600 elem) of single-host eval-MACHINERY cost, additive to and separate from the composition prize. Mechanism: share the ~70% re-collection half of the 4,559-calls/element cost by collecting the submodule shape once. REQUIRES the engine to OWN extendModules (wrapper injection lost on every extend; verified).
whatChanges.magnitude: Measured: 4,559 fn-calls/element, ~67% hoistable lower bound. MUST STILL MEASURE: net delivered % after cache overhead + default-prepend fast-path-miss; byte-identical nix-diff of system.build.toplevel both ways.

whatChanges.item: The merge kernel (H7) — does a NEW gen-merge close the gap
whatChanges.phase1Said: Retaining nixpkgs option content forces type-driven merge (mkOverride+mkIf+mkOrder+type.merge) to run somewhere; NO gen primitive reproduces it (foldLayers priority-blind/positional, gen-derive resolves rules not values); the merge can only be relocated/shared, never eliminated.
whatChanges.expandedScopeChangesItTo: H7 NARROWED: merge SEMANTICS stay intrinsic (must run, byte-observable), but the merge IMPLEMENTATION moves from machinery-we-host to code-we-own. A literature-grounded gen-merge (Radul merge-lattice CONTRACT + Datafun monotone-semilattice CONVERGENCE + Leijen scoped-label def-STACK) CAN reproduce the algebra — no single paper founds it, it is a synthesis. delta-nets does NOT found it (declined). NEW obligation: the v2 dual interface + isV2MergeCoherent.
whatChanges.magnitude: Verified non-positional live (['c'] for force-mid-list; stable order-sort). MUST MEASURE: byte-identical parity across the full wrapper×type cross-product INCLUDING merge.v2; per-option interpretation overhead (a Nix-level dispatch may be 3-10x the native fast path → net loss risk on single-host).

whatChanges.item: Sound incremental override on VANILLA nixpkgs modules (runtime read-capture)
whatChanges.phase1Said: H4: module config.* read-set is NOT statically recoverable (functionArgs name-only, dynamic config.${k} selects, doRename aliasing, with). Static partitioning of vanilla modules below the per-host boundary is impossible; partitioning must be contract-based.
whatChanges.expandedScopeChangesItTo: Read-capture moves from IMPOSSIBLE-statically to POSSIBLE-dynamically-but-out-of-band. A custom engine controlling config construction (applyModuleArgs seam) OBSERVES the real read-set via trace-on-force (sound+complete at forced-leaf granularity), defeating H4's obstacles because it observes rather than predicts. Builds adios-style reverse edges → region/host-granular incremental override on opaque modules.
whatChanges.magnitude: Verified: traces survive the full nixpkgs merge and fire at real cross-module consumers. MUST MEASURE: spine-rewrap overhead on a 2047-module host (may swamp the saving); whether it beats adios's DECLARED-inputs contract given vanilla=coarse anyway. BOUNDED by H6 (intra-eval; cross-invocation needs out-of-language store) and the stale-manifest under-invalidation risk.

whatChanges.phase1Said: gen-schema/gen-aspects are built ON lib.types.deferredModule and force lib.evalModules on introspection (entry-type.nix:32,:223); gen as-shipped ADDS module machinery. The parent/collection graph plane is already module-free; only ref/option introspection forces evalModules.
whatChanges.expandedScopeChangesItTo: H5 DISSOLVES under the expanded scope. Because we control the deferredModule contract, gen introspection targets the SAME custom evalModules — the module-free gen graph plane and the host module plane become ONE engine sharing one injection point (lib.modules.evalModules). The contract can be changed to call a faster shape-only path (gen-lazy eval-one) directly. The 'gen adds machinery' problem is removed.
whatChanges.magnitude: Empirically: reading output-modules forces ZERO evalModules; materializing one host forces ONLY that host's. MUST MEASURE: the cost of inverting the dependency direction (gen built ON deferredModule at entry-type.nix:32) — a real refactor, unestimated.

whatChanges.item: Cost center A (eager shape transpose) attackability
whatChanges.phase1Said: A is the dominant single-host floor, eager regardless of which config attr is read (zipAttrs transpose forces attrNames at every nesting level). Intrinsic under verbatim lib.
whatChanges.expandedScopeChangesItTo: PARTIALLY ATTACKABLE: gen-lazy first-order constructors make the per-path declsByName entry demand-driven, and the per-element re-payment inside submodules is hoistable (the D mechanism). The zipAttrs KEY-UNION stays intrinsic (unmatched-check/freeform need it; H3/H4). So the 601K single-host floor is shareable-across-elements but not removable-per-level.
whatChanges.magnitude: Verified: 601,273 fn-calls to read one leaf ≈ forcing attrNames (601,201). MUST MEASURE: whether the transpose can be made lazy-in-attrNames without forcing the spine, and whether that survives the dynamic-select reality (can't know a module's paths without importing it).

whatChanges.phase1Said: B is a skippable eager full-attrName walk, gated via check=false/freeformType; ~3.9% machinery.
whatChanges.expandedScopeChangesItTo: CONFIRMED MACHINERY, MORE attackable: under a custom engine it need never be BUILT (not merely skipped) — the unmatchedDefns second walk disappears entirely for trusted leaves, folded into lazy A. The freeform-modules split (PR #82743) is a ready compat-preserving precedent to copy.
whatChanges.magnitude: Verified FIXED 110,035 fn-calls/level, does not scale with N. Fully realizable; modest single-host but compounds per-submodule-TYPE with D-hoisting.

whatChanges.phase1Said: H1: the //-storm is intrinsic (abandoning the module system moves it −0.3%); ~1.756M copies from import<nixpkgs>. H6: pure Nix cannot persist a forced thunk across nix-eval invocations.
whatChanges.expandedScopeChangesItTo: UNCHANGED — both survive a full custom engine verbatim. H1 is the one hard floor regardless of engine (submodule recursion contributes 0.017%/element to copies). H6 bounds ALL incrementality to intra-eval; cross-host/cross-CLI requires an out-of-language store outside the pure-framework scope. The reframe attacks A/B/D (machinery), never the copy floor or cross-process persistence.
whatChanges.magnitude: No measurement needed — these are confirmed floors. hola must measure ALL wins NET of the fixed ~1.85M-copy / ~2.69M-fn-call base.

result.parityVerdict:
TRACTABLE but LARGE with a long correctness tail; lib-injection + continuous nix-diff dual-run is NECESSARY and (for the success path) SUFFICIENT to de-risk — but NOT sufficient for failure-mode parity.

INJECTION COMPLETENESS (verified end-to-end): lib threads all the way down via makeExtensible+callLibs (default.nix:48) — nixosSystem(flake.nix:65)→eval-config.nix(:32)→evalModules→types all receive the patched lib. The minimal override set is exactly TWO attrs in ONE lib.extend overlay: modules.evalModules (the engine) + the top-level evalModules alias (=final.modules.evalModules); submoduleWith picks up the engine for free via `inherit (lib.modules) evalModules` (types.nix:1375). NO nixpkgs fork; modules stay verbatim. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: overriding lib.types.submoduleWith/submodule/attrsOf/lazyAttrsOf/deferredModule via extend is a NO-OP (they call LOCAL bindings, types.nix:1277,1304,1489; only functor.type at :1499 uses the lib-qualified one — verified customSubmoduleUsed=false). extendModules (modules.nix:386) calls the CLOSED-OVER LOCAL evalModules, so a WRAPPER-style override is LOST on every extend (verified extendedPostmark=false). CONSEQUENCE: to attack D you must REPLACE the modules.nix body so your engine owns extendModules and recurses into ITSELF — a thin wrapper is structurally insufficient.

MINIMAL 80/20 SUBSET (frequency-anchored over nixos/modules): (1) five property wrappers mkIf(5034)/mkDefault(1832)/mkMerge(553)/mkForce(107)/mkOverride(70) + mkOptionDefault + the pushDownProperties/dischargeProperties/filterOverrides/sortProperties discharge; (2) ~12 types bool/int/str/enum(594)/nullOr(1997)/listOf(1459)/attrsOf(646)/lazyAttrsOf(13)/submoduleWith(886)/either(103)/coercedTo(80)/freeformType(525) with BOTH legacy AND v2 merge; (3) flat config fixpoint + _module.args/specialArgs via name-only functionArgs reflection; (4) mergeOptionDecls split-declaration merge + optionType type-merge; (5) extendModules as a STABLE primitive (load-bearing USER-FACING surface — specialisation.nix:56-83 builds a sub-config TYPE from it); (6) the doRename/apply rename-forward path (mkRenamedOptionModule 762 + mkRemovedOptionModule 759 = 1521 CROSS-MODULE config/options readers). Tail you can stub/defer: attrTag(1), mkMergedOptionModule(3), deferredModule(3), doc-path defaultText/example, error-message provenance.

THREE SILENT-MIS-EVALUATION LANDMINES (the go/no-go; each type-checks clean and ships a derivation that builds but DIFFERS): L1 high-priority whole-attrset def EVAPORATES normal-priority sibling keys (`mkForce {a=99;}` next to `{a=1;b=2;}` → {a:99} NOT {a:99;b:2} — filterOverrides picks highestPrio over the WHOLE-option def list BEFORE attrsOf descends; a naive merge-then-override-per-key engine produces {a:99;b:2}). L2 attrsOf-vs-lazyAttrsOf mkIf-false strictness DIVERGENCE flips `cfg ? attr` (deliberate, types.nix:1013-1021). L3 the v2-merge headError/isV2MergeCoherent triple (get the triple shape wrong → modern containers type-check clean but merge wrong). RE-VERIFIED LIVE: merge is non-positional — `[normal,mkForce,mkDefault,mkIf false,mkOrder 10]` → ["c"]; `[mkAfter,normal,mkBefore]` → ["first","mid","last"]. foldLayers' positional strategies provably cannot model this.

DUAL-RUN HARNESS (verified & demonstrated): realLib.evalModules vs customLib.evalModules over ONE shared module set in ONE eval → configDiff=[], enginesDistinct=true, optionNamesEqual=true, with mkForce/mkOrder/attrsOf-submodule/name-arg all resolving identically; deep introspection parity holds (getSubOptions identical). Extends to the option TYPE plane for gen-schema parity. Makes the reimplementation testable INCREMENTALLY (swap engine, keep diff green) not big-bang.

SUFFICIENCY VERDICT: lib-injection + continuous nix-diff (config plane + getSubOptions introspection plane) + a system.build.toplevel drvPath nix-diff against vanilla lib.nixosSystem is SUFFICIENT to de-risk the SUCCESS path and MANDATORY before trusting the engine. NOT sufficient for FAILURE-mode parity (error messages embed _file/key/declarationPositions/showDefs/levenshtein suggestions); the provenance-fidelity bar must be decided separately. RESIDUAL LEAK: the nixpkgsConfig eval (pkgs/top-level/default.nix:140) runs on the package-set's OWN lib (intrinsic //-storm, ~99 copies, H1) — "inject the engine" ≠ "every evalModules in the closure is yours"; home-manager/nix-darwin each build their own evalModules chains needing the patched lib per-framework (unmapped).
result.hardFloor:
A full HOAG custom engine STILL does not change four things, and the honest upside is bounded by them.

(1) THE //-STORM (H1) is the one hard floor. Abandoning the entire module system moves it −0.3% (1,851,228→1,846,322 copies single-system); it is the package-set fixpoint (fixed-points.nix:333 extends), forced by import<nixpkgs>{} (~1,756,268 copies BEFORE any output is forced, +99 for evalModules). Submodule recursion — the big per-host prize — contributes only 0.017%/element (1,911 of an 11.2M base) to copies. gen-merge/gen-lazy/gen-trace touch module MACHINERY (fn-calls/thunks/envs), not import<nixpkgs>. Every evaluator-level precedent (Lix 2.94 string reuse + parallel GC, Determinate parallel eval, Tvix bytecode) confirms this is the only lever on the copy bulk and it lives BELOW the module system. hola must measure ALL wins NET of this fixed ~1.85M-copy / ~2.69M-fn-call base.

(2) MERGE SEMANTICS MUST RUN SOMEWHERE (H7). Retaining nixpkgs option content forces the type-driven merge (mkOverride priority MIN-fold + mkIf discharge + mkOrder stable-sort + type.merge submodule recursion, both legacy AND v2 triple) at every conflict site. A custom engine reimplements the IMPLEMENTATION (fuse the pipeline, maximize single-def fast-path hits) but CANNOT change the SEMANTICS — byte-observable (L1/L2/L3). The measured cost of an extra merged def is only ~67 calls/element, so this floor is SMALL — but non-removable, and a Nix-level gen-merge (interpreted tagged-data dispatch) may be 3-10x SLOWER PER OPTION than nixpkgs' builtin-heavy native fast path (modules.nix:1214-1235), risking a NET LOSS on single-host even while winning composition. UNMEASURED — needs a microbenchmark before committing.

(3) IMPORT-CLOSURE FORCING is irreducible. Reading one trivial NixOS leaf forces 601,273 fn-calls (≈ forcing attrNames config, 601,201) — ~all of it import-closure + eager zipAttrs KEY-UNION, which a custom engine can hoist across submodule elements and demand-drive per-name VALUE subtree but CANNOT prune (H3 flat non-statically-partitionable namespace; H4 read-set not statically recoverable). The single-host transpose floor is shareable-across-elements, not removable-per-level. doRename/mkRenamedOptionModule/mkRemovedOptionModule (1521 CROSS-MODULE config/options readers) are the practical refutation of any per-module partitioning below the per-host/submodule boundary — the engine must run a whole-config fixpoint.

(4) NO CROSS-PROCESS PERSISTENCE (H6) — the floor on incrementality. Pure Nix cannot persist a forced thunk across nix-eval invocations (functions non-serializable; toJSON/toString of a lambda throws; flake eval-cache keys only locked-rev leaf values). ALL runtime read-capture and adios-style reverse-edge incremental override is INTRA-EVAL only. Cross-host/cross-CLI incrementality REQUIRES an out-of-language store (IFD / content-addressed cache / sidecar trace / Lix plugin) — outside the pure-framework scope. The read-manifest must be re-derived each run or stored out-of-language. Incremental override is config-coupling-bounded: the moment a delivery edge or pipe.withConfig crosses into `config`, the consuming host's evalModules fixpoint re-couples and laziness is lost.

HONEST UPSIDE BOUND: realizable wins are (a) adios-flake-class ~28-30% on the composition/multi-system axis (the ONLY axis Phase 1 measured); PLUS, NEWLY, (b) a per-host D-axis win worth ~30% (server) to ~71% (heavy host) of single-host eval-MACHINERY cost, additive to (a). Both are bounded BELOW by H1 (copy floor untouched) and the value-merge slice of D (~13-34%, intrinsic). The net delivered per-host number is < the 70% ceiling after cache overhead and the gen-merge per-option interpretation tax — UNMEASURED. Do not inflate the reframe past: "per-host evalModules CAN be made meaningfully cheaper, proportional to submodule-element density, on the machinery slice only."

candidates.name: Custom lazy HOAG evalModules owning extendModules (submodule shared-base hoisting)
candidates.thesis: Replace the modules.nix evalModules body with a custom engine that collects+transposes the submodule declaration shape ONCE per submodule TYPE and per-element runs only leaf mergeDefinitions — eliminating the ~70% re-collection half of the D-axis cost that verbatim lib re-pays per element.
candidates.mechanism: The engine defines its own extendModules pointing at ITSELF (not the closed-over local), caches the (matchedOptions skeleton, option types, default-def prepends) keyed on the shared regularModules (provably element-independent: closed over submoduleWith.modules, defs arrive only via ++allModules defs; types.nix:1452, modules.nix:386). Per element only the leaves with an actual per-element definition run mergeDefinitions. Installed as final.modules.evalModules + the evalModules alias in one lib.extend overlay; submoduleWith routes into it via `inherit (lib.modules) evalModules`.
candidates.enablesWhat: The FIRST measured per-host win: ~30% of base eval-machinery on a server (~250 submodule elements), ~71% on a heavy host (~600). Additive to and separate from the composition/multi-system prize. Requires owning extendModules — wrapper-style injection is structurally insufficient (verified extendedPostmark=false).
candidates.risk: Must produce BYTE-IDENTICAL config (nix-diff system.build.toplevel both ways) — the freeform/unmatched-defn partition interacts with the hoisted shape and must be re-derived per element; mkOptionDefault default-prepend pushes >=2 defs onto many leaves, possibly raising the merge floor above the measured 13-34% and shrinking the hoistable fraction; cache construction+lookup overhead nets the 70% ceiling to an unmeasured delivered number.

candidates.name: gen-merge — literature-grounded priority-aware type-driven merge primitive
candidates.thesis: The ONE primitive Phase 1 said does not exist: a pure-Nix module implementing mkOverride/mkIf/mkOrder/mkMerge/type.merge byte-identically to lib/types, synthesized from three papers (no single paper founds the algebra).
candidates.mechanism: Radul-2009 propagator merge-lattice supplies the generic-merge CONTRACT (merge :: old→new→{redundant|superseded|fresh|contradiction}, cells own the lattice; mkOverride priority IS a total-order lattice, filterOverrides IS the superseded-select). Datafun (arntzenius-2016) supplies the monotone-finite-height-semilattice CONVERGENCE guarantee for the recursive submodule fixpoint. Leijen-2005 scoped-labels (already gen-algebra rec.nix shadow-stack) is the per-option def-STACK substrate. Wrappers are defunctionalized tagged-data CONSTRUCTORS (Reynolds/Lorenzen — inspectable). mergeDefs :: type → [taggedDef] → value implements discharge→priorityFilter→stableSort→typeMerge in that FIXED order.
candidates.enablesWhat: Closes the H7 gap — the merge IMPLEMENTATION becomes code hola owns. Must satisfy laws L1 priority-dominance (def at prio p annihilates ALL defs at prio>p irrespective of position — NOT last-wins), L2 condition-evaporation (mkIf false ≡ absence), L3 orthogonal stable order-key, L4 single-def fast path (keeps single-def leaves ~free). Plus the NEW v2 dual interface (legacy merge + merge.v2 {headError,value,valueMeta} triple + check as __functor w/ isV2MergeCoherent — verified live on listOf str).
candidates.risk: BYTE-IDENTICAL parity across the full mkOverride/mkIf/mkOrder/mkMerge cross-product INCLUDING merge.v2 coherence + emptyValue/mkOptionDefault prepend is an implementation HAZARD. Per-option interpretation overhead of a Nix-level dispatch may be 3-10x the native fast path → NET LOSS on single-host even while winning composition. delta-nets (salvadori-2025) does NOT found this — its fan annihilate/duplicate is λ-sharing, backs only C1 prefix-sharing; forcing that citation is an overclaim, declined.

candidates.name: gen-lazy — first-order lazy constructors for demand-driven shape (attacks A)
candidates.thesis: Convert the O(#modules × #option-paths) EAGER zipAttrs transpose into a demand-scaled one: build the declsByName/pushedDownDefinitions entry for an option-path only when that path is read, and let the gen graph plane read option SHAPE without running merge.
candidates.mechanism: Lorenzen-2025 typed first-order lazy constructors (tagged attrsets with __lazy markers; inspect reads fields without forcing; force; eval-one forces exactly one level). Each option-path's def-list is a lazy constructor MergeAt(path,[defs]) not forced until config.<path> is demanded. eval-one lets the graph plane check top-level option declarations without evaluating nested imports (the deferredModule today is all-or-nothing).
candidates.enablesWhat: Folds cost B (check) into lazy A (only walk shape for paths actually read), makes per-name resultsByName demand-driven, lets gen introspection read which paths exist without forcing merge.
candidates.risk: Nix is ALREADY lazy — the win is making the transpose lazy IN ATTRNAMES (today zipAttrs/genericClosure force the spine), not 'add laziness'. The ASYMPTOTIC floor survives (must touch every module to know its declared paths; H3/H4) — the win is constant-factor + inspectable-before-forcing, possibly smaller than the mapping suggests. Cannot soundly demand-drive the NAME SET (would miss unmatched defs / break checkUnmatched).

candidates.name: Runtime read-capture incremental override on VANILLA modules (trace-on-force)
candidates.thesis: Defeat H4's static-impossibility by OBSERVING rather than predicting: a custom engine controlling config construction recovers the SOUND+COMPLETE leaf read-set of unmodified nixpkgs modules dynamically.
candidates.mechanism: Pass each module a TAGGED tracing VIEW of config via applyModuleArgs (modules.nix:707-739). Leaves are builtins.trace 'READ:<path>' value — fires iff the thunk is forced (a read IS a force), survives the full nixpkgs merge pipeline and fires at the real cross-module consumer demand point (verified). hola's driver parses stderr into a read-manifest. Builds adios-style reverse edges → invalidate only hosts/regions whose read-set intersects a changed option. Clean substrate: a Lix custom builtin / force-hook (env runs Lix 2.96) replacing the stderr hack with structured per-access capture, no spine-rewrap, no !(v?_type) guard.
candidates.enablesWhat: REGION/HOST-granular incremental override on opaque vanilla modules (strictly coarser than adios's per-module, strictly finer than re-eval-everything). adios resolveTree/diff but with OBSERVED edges substituted for adios's DECLARED inputs.
candidates.risk: Two-process / out-of-band ONLY — stderr cannot be reabsorbed in-eval (pure single-pass value-capture impossible without rewriting modules into a writer monad, defeating 'hosts unmodified'). Cross-invocation persistence needs out-of-language store (H6). Granularity bounded by wrap boundary = submodule/per-host leaf (H3); coarse reads over-capture (sound, incomplete). Stale manifest can UNDER-invalidate if an edit adds a read the old manifest didn't see. Spine-rewrap is an A-class eager cost on a 2047-module host — UNMEASURED, may swamp the saving. Open: does it beat adios's DECLARED-inputs contract, given vanilla = coarse anyway?

candidates.name: gen-trace + applicativeAttr — verifying-traces rebuilder + sound read-barrier
candidates.thesis: Land hola in Mokhov-2018's (Suspending, Verifying-Traces)=Shake cell for the option graph: record per-attribute (sourceId,attr,contentHash), skip recompute when dependency hashes match, and enforce a sound cross-region read-barrier.
candidates.mechanism: Mokhov scheduler/rebuilder decomposition — Nix is the Suspending scheduler, gen-scope _eval the 'done' set; the MISSING rebuilder is verifying-traces. applicativeAttr (Mokhov Task Applicative + Const [k] static dep extraction) declares its dependency set statically → a read-barrier proxy that THROWS on an undeclared per-system-input read (the C1-prefix-sharing soundness guard, Phase-1 Q5). Radul v&s provenance ({value; support=premiseSet}) gives both the dependency graph AND 'why does this option have this value' attribution.
candidates.enablesWhat: Early-cutoff + minimal incremental re-eval intra-eval (share system-independent prefix, dedup host/input forces); enforceable contract for hola-native edge-annotated modules.
candidates.risk: INTRA-eval only (H6). The H4 soundness kill is UNCHANGED — auto-discovery on verbatim nixpkgs stays unsound/conservative; the read-barrier protects only CONTRACT-annotated edges, leaving vanilla-module prefix-sharing unguarded (fail-open-to-prefix). Per-option provenance has known overhead (Radul S7) — may have to be opt-in. Datafun monotone-fixpoint soundness is threatened by mkIf with config-dependent (non-monotone) conditions — may need stratification (gen-derive phases) over the condition-dependency graph.

candidates.name: Module-free gen introspection unifying the two planes (H5 dissolved)
candidates.thesis: Because we control the gen-schema/gen-aspects deferredModule contract, point gen introspection at the CUSTOM evalModules — the module-free gen graph plane and the host module plane become ONE engine, removing the 'gen adds machinery' problem Phase 1 identified.
candidates.mechanism: gen-schema's parent/collection graph plane is ALREADY module-free (entry-type.nix:89-97 pure foldl'); only ref/option introspection forces lib.evalModules (entry-type.nix:32 base=deferredModule, :223). The deferredModule contract routes through the SAME lib.modules.evalModules seam, so under a custom engine the introspection eval is automatically the custom engine's — no extra shim. Change the contract to call a faster introspection path (gen-lazy eval-one for shape-only) directly.
candidates.enablesWhat: gen introspection stops forcing full lib.evalModules; the off-module HOAG graph plane (nodes=entity scopes, edges=P/I/delivery S,T,P,M over MODULE LISTS) and the terminal merge share one injection point. Per-host demand skipping + reverse-edge incremental override become expressible where a flat fixpoint cannot.
candidates.risk: The dependency DIRECTION is inverted (gen built ON lib.types.deferredModule at entry-type.nix:32) — a real refactor of unestimated cost. The HOAG graph reaches modules and STOPS at the module-list boundary; it never reaches options, so it does NOT by itself attack A/B/D — those need the separately-built C1/C2/C3. Naming module-list edges is orthogonal to per-option merge. Confirmed empirically: reading output-modules forces ZERO evalModules; materializing one host forces ONLY that host's. The graph buys composition-axis incrementality (~30%, the measured win), NOT a cheaper single-host merge.

claim.claim: The per-element cost of attrsOf/listOf(submodule) is ~66-67% (fn-calls/thunks) to 87% (//-copies) RE-COLLECTION and ~13-34% VALUE-MERGE, measured two independent ways (vary-decls and vary-merge-layers) that AGREE; lower-bound hoistable = ~67% on fn-calls. On a real eval-config.nix host each systemd.service costs (3,142,584−2,691,243)/99 = 4,559 fn-calls / 6,500 thunks marginally, FLAT 1→100, with copies only 1,911/element (0.017% of the 11.2M base). FALSIFIABLE: re-run /tmp/hola-exp/realsvc.nix and /tmp/hola-exp/dual.nix.
claim.whyLoadBearing: This is the entire per-host-prize thesis. If re-collection is NOT the dominant half, the D-axis win collapses and Phase 1's 'no per-host mechanism' verdict stands. The measurement is what flips H2 from 'multi-system axis only' to 'multi-system PLUS per-host D'.

verdicts.nuance: The claim's DIRECTIONAL core is true and reproducible: per-element attrsOf-submodule cost is re-collection-DOMINATED, not value-merge-dominated. But the PRECISE headline — "66-67% re-collection / 13-34% value-merge, measured two independent ways that AGREE, lower-bound hoistable = ~67%" — is not soundly identified by the cited experiments and is refuted on four source-grounded grounds. (1) The two methods do NOT agree on a single split; they disagree by metric: value-merge is ~19% (fn) / ~13% (thunks) / 0% (copies) at 6 layers. The "87% re-collection on copies" is just "merge is FREE in copies" (0 merge copies/layer, verified), not a corroborating measurement of the fn/thunk split. (2) The "vary-merge-layers" arm (dual.nix) is semantically wrong for value-merge: elemVal sets opt0 = mkOverride(100-l) for l=0..5, so filterOverrides' (modules.nix:1415-1436, highestPrio=min) keeps exactly ONE definition and DISCARDS the other five before type.merge ever runs — verified, 6 layers yields "v0.5", the rest evaporate. It measures dischargeProperties+filterOverrides' override-DISCARD overhead, not type.merge of coexisting defs. A corrected experiment with distinct coexisting options (dual2.nix) gives a similar per-layer slope (~76 fn) but confirms collection still dominates. (3) Collection and the per-option mergeDefinitions fast-path (modules.nix:1214-1235) are NOT separable by these runs — both scale with #decls and are bundled in the 48-fn/decl vary-decls slope — so the percentages are an artifact of which zero-merge baseline you assume; at the realistic operating point (one def/option, L1) collection is 95-97%, and "66-67%" only appears under a worst-case 6-override-layer attribution. (4) The real-config vary-merge arm (mergeload.nix) actually FAILED to evaluate at layers=2 and 4 (conflicting-definition throw, modules.nix:1147), so the "two independent ways" reduce to one synthetic experiment plus a non-decomposing per-element marginal. The realsvc.nix arithmetic itself is EXACT and reproducible (4,559 fn / 6,500 thunks / 1,911 copies per svc, FLAT 1->100 confirmed to the unit; copies 0.0170% of base) and identical-vs-distinct services cost byte-identically (3,142,584 fn either way — nixpkgs never dedups per-element submodule collection), which genuinely supports "re-collection is the repeated, in-principle-hoistable dominant half." But that supports a coarser claim ("collection dominates and is repeated per element") than the precise, falsely-corroborated 66-67%/13-34% figure the brief asserts. Net for the D-axis thesis: the qualitative finding that re-collection is the dominant, hoistable half SURVIVES and the per-host prize plausibly exists; the specific load-bearing percentages and the "two methods agree" rigor claim do NOT.
verdicts.evidence: Re-ran both falsifiable experiments against nixpkgs @2f4f625e (2026-06-23). realsvc.nix reproduces exactly: n=1 fn=2,691,243 / n=100 fn=3,142,584 -> (3,142,584-2,691,243)/99=4,559.0 fn, 6,500.0 thunks, 1,911.0 copies per svc, perfectly flat across 0->1->50->100 (verified per-step). dual.nix reproduces exactly: d_1_20_1 fn=1925, d_50_20_1 fn=73073, d_1_20_6 fn=2259, d_50_20_6 fn=89773. Per-element @L1=1452 fn, @L6=1786 fn; value-merge delta-share @L6 = 18.7% fn / 13.0% thunk / 0.0% copies (NOT a single 13-34% split; methods disagree by metric). filterOverrides' min-priority discard verified at modules.nix:1415-1436; mkMerge of mkOverride(100-l) for l=0..5 -> result "v0.5" (5 of 6 defs discarded pre-type.merge), so dual.nix's layers axis does not measure type.merge. mergeload.nix (real-config vary-merge arm) throws at layers=2/4: "option systemd.services.svc0.description has conflicting definition values" (modules.nix:1147) — ml_err_50_2.txt/ml_err_50_4.txt; only layers=1 succeeded, so that arm never decomposed. vary-decls slope = 48 fn/decl + 492 fixed per element (ndecls 5/20/40 -> 732/1452/2412 per-elem), and this slope bundles declaration-collection with per-option mergeDefinitions fast-path (modules.nix:1214-1235), making the collection/merge split non-identifiable. Hoistability probe (ident.nix): 100 identical vs 100 distinct services both cost fn=3,142,584 / thunks=4,926,860 — zero saving, confirming nixpkgs runs a fresh per-element evalModules via extendModules (types.nix:1452-1455 -> modules.nix:380-393) with no content dedup, so the repeated re-collection is real and is the structural target a custom HOAG engine would share. Corrected merge experiment dual2.nix (distinct coexisting options, all forced): per-layer merge ~76 fn / 64 thunks, collection still dominant (per-elem L1 ~1490 fn). verdicts.nuance: HC1 has two layers; the RAW MEASUREMENT layer reproduces exactly, the DECOMPOSITION/HOISTABILITY layer fails. Reproduced exactly from re-running /tmp/hola-exp/realsvc.nix at n=1/50/100: per systemd.service = (3,142,584-2,691,243)/99 = 4,559 fn-calls, 6,500 thunks, 1,911 copies, FLAT 1->100, copies = 0.017% of the 11.2M base. That part is solid and falsifiable-confirmed. What is REFUTED is "~66-67% re-collection / ~13-34% value-merge, measured two independent ways that AGREE; lower-bound hoistable ~67% on fn-calls." Three independent failures. (1) The "two independent ways" are NOT independent: the real-config vary-merge method (mergeload.nix) ERRORS for every layers>1 — both layers set systemd.services.svcK.description with lib.mkDefault, producing "conflicting definition values" at modules.nix:1147 (two equal-priority defaults do not merge). Only the SYNTHETIC dual.nix produced both axes, so "two independent ways" is two axes of one synthetic file. (2) The value-merge fraction is not a fixed property — it is a knob: dual.nix's per-layer merge slope is ~67 fn/layer, so the "merge %" you read off is 1*67/total at +1 layer (~5%) but 5*67/total at +5 layers (~19%); the 13-34% band just reflects how many mkOverride layers the experimenter stacked, not a property of real configs. (3) Most decisive — a new ndecls-demand-split experiment (this study, /tmp/hola-exp/ndecls_scale.nix + demand.nix) shows the per-declared-option per-element cost is 48 fn when every option is deepSeq'd but only 11 fn when only opt0 is forced; the other ~37 is DEMAND-DRIVEN single-def per-option merge (evalOptionValue->mergeDefinitions->mkOptionDefault prepend->filterOverrides'->type.merge, modules.nix:1100-1311, fast path :1214-1235), i.e. the intrinsic H7 merge KERNEL, not hoistable re-collection. The claim's "vary-decls residual = re-collection" inference silently reclassifies this intrinsic single-def merge as re-collection, inflating the hoistable fraction. On the REAL systemd path this is confirmed structurally: per-service cost scales with options DEMANDED (1,630 fn at k=1 -> 2,085 fn at k=6 forced options; realsvc_opts.nix) and forcing only the name+one description costs just 19 fn/service (realsvc_demand.nix) — so the 4,559 is dominated by demand-driven per-option merge/render of the unit options .text touches, not by a fixed eager re-collection that could be hoisted once. A genuine hoistable eager-shape component DOES exist (each element's extendModules at types.nix:1452 re-runs collectModules+mergeModules' zipAttrs shape transpose at modules.nix:789-808 over the element-independent submodule option set, and that eager force is ~11 fn/declared-option independent of demand) — but it is SMALLER than 67% and is not what dual.nix's vary-decls/vary-merge "agreement" measures. WHERE IT HOLDS: if "re-collection" is redefined as only the eager declsByName shape transpose (element-independent, hoistable) and "value-merge" as all demand-driven evalOptionValue work (intrinsic), the split flips toward merge being the larger half on real demanded configs, collapsing the D-axis per-host prize and leaving Phase 1's "no per-host mechanism" verdict standing. The claim as written — 67% hoistable, dual-method agreement — does not survive.

Marginal per service: 4,559 fn / 6,500 th / 1,911 cp, flat. EXACT match to HC1. claim.claim: A custom HOAG engine adds a SECOND, per-host win on the D axis worth ~30% (server ~250 submodule elements) to ~71% (heavy host ~600) of single-host eval-MACHINERY cost (fn-calls/thunks), additive to and separate from the composition/multi-system ~28-30% prize — but ONLY if the engine OWNS extendModules (a wrapper-style injection is lost on every extend; verified extendedPostmark=false). FALSIFIABLE: the prize evaporates if a real shared-base engine's cache overhead + the mkOptionDefault default-prepend pushing leaves off the single-def fast path nets the 70% ceiling below the composition-axis baseline.
claim.whyLoadBearing: THE verdict delta vs Phase 1. Phase 1: 'orchestrate-around-verbatim, ~0 per-host.' Expanded scope: per-host evalModules CAN be made meaningfully cheaper, proportional to submodule-element density, on the machinery slice. The 70% is a CEILING not a delivered number — the prototype must net it out.

verdicts.evidence: EXPERIMENTS (NIX_SHOW_STATS, nix-instantiate --eval --strict, nixpkgs HEAD): (1) SHARED-SHAPE FLOOR vs VANILLA N=250 B=20: simulated shared-shape engine (collect base option-tree once, per-element evalOptionValue against shared decls) = 111,159 fn-calls vs vanilla attrsOf(submodule) 358,723 => 69% reduction, in HC2's 30-71% band. (2) CORRECTNESS byte-identical: equal=true, shaped_e0==vanilla_e0=={opt0=set0;opt1=d1;opt2=d2;opt3=d3}. (3) NO BASE MEMOIZATION: BASE-FN-RUN trace fired 5x for 5 elements. (4) WRAPPER LOST AT SUBMODULE BOUNDARY: outer specialArgs.postmark absent (SAW-SPECIALARGS: empty). (5) SHAPE DOMINATES: force=options 321,314 vs force=config 358,723 (shape=90%). (6) mkOptionDefault FAST-PATH PENALTY: every-option-set with-default 472,973 vs no-default-fast-path 342,973 (~26 fn-calls/option). (7) LINEARITY: N=1->1,961 N=250->382,433 N=500->764,433 (1,528/element); B=0->114,430 B=10->257,433 B=40->632,433. SOURCE lib/modules.nix:380-393 extendModules re-enters top-level evalModules over regularModules++modules, no key on regularModules; :259-275 doCollect+mergeModules re-run per call; :1104-1114 evalOptionValue prepends mkOptionDefault=mkOverride 1500 (:1567,:1562); :1221-1235 fast path keys length==1 && !(isAttrs && ?_type). lib/types.nix:1393-1416 base=evalModules once, :1445-1461 merge calls base.extendModules per element, :1370/:1380-1391 shorthandOnlyDefinesConfig default false. nixos/lib/systemd-types.nix:131-227 services/timers/sockets=attrsOf(submodule[...]) no shorthandOnly. extendModules consumers: specialisation.nix:60, build-vm.nix:13-17, images.nix:60, facter/debug.nix:5, eval-config.nix:132/141 (destructure _module.args.extendModules, modules.nix:249). evalOptionValue emits external-use-deprecated warning. 47 'default = name' sites. HOLDS for pure-config-shorthand elements with name-independent defaults; FAILS for module-carrying elements (verified e1 declaring options.y=99 works) and name-dependent defaults.
verdicts.nuance: HC2 UPHELD as a real, per-host, D-axis-proportional win, but 71% is a heavily-CONDITIONED ceiling and the soundness/compat cost is larger than stated. MECHANISM REAL AND NUMBER LANDS: simulated shared-shape engine = 111,159 vs vanilla 358,723 fn-calls (N=250/B=20 server-class) = 69% reduction, in HC2's 30-71% band at the heavy end, byte-identical (equal=true) so it preserves the H7 merge kernel. 'MUST OWN extendModules' CONFIRMED: a wrapper at the outer evalModules is LOST at the submodule boundary because base (types.nix:1393-1394) captures specialArgs from submoduleWith def-site closure not the outer call (measured outer postmark NOT reaching element); base module-function re-runs once-per-element, zero memoization (BASE-FN-RUN 5x for 5 elements). COST LOCATION: forcing only per-element option SHAPE (no values) = 321,314 = 90% of full -- the dominant slice is per-element re-construction of the SAME shared declaration shape = genuinely shareable. FALSIFICATION CLAUSES DO NOT DEFEAT IT: mkOptionDefault default-prepend penalty is real/measured (~26 fn-calls/option off the :1221-1235 length==1 fast path because :1104-1114 prepends mkOverride 1500 making defs' length>=2 and _type-bearing) BUT lives INSIDE the 111K shared-shape floor; the 69% is net of it. WHERE REFUTATION BITES (clean ceiling -> conditioned): (1) module-carrying elements defeat base-sharing -- a per-element def can be a full module declaring NEW options (verified e1 with options.y), and systemd.services/sockets/timers are attrsOf(submodule[...]) WITHOUT shorthandOnlyDefinesConfig (types.nix:1370 default false), so the canonical D-axis workload PERMITS elements that must re-collect; only pure-config shorthand shares. (2) name-dependent defaults (47 'default = name' sites) re-eval per element, not shareable. (3) compat cost: owning extendModules means owning submoduleWith AND the _module.args.extendModules function-value contract real modules destructure and call (specialisation inheritParentConfig, build-vm, images, facter, eval-config); evalOptionValue (the reused merge kernel) is OFFICIALLY DEPRECATED for external use, an unstable internal contract. NET: prize real, additive to composition-axis ~28-30%, proportional to (element density x base-option count); DELIVERED fraction gated by share of pure-config-shorthand elements with name-independent defaults. HC2's own 'CEILING not delivered, prototype must net it out' caveat is exactly right and load-bearing.

UPHELD as written — it is correctly framed as a CEILING conditional on owning extendModules and on netting out, and it survives the soundness attack — but with three load-bearing qualifications the prototype must honor. (1) The 30-71% is a fraction of the MACHINERY slice, not of total eval; in a real NixOS host the submodule recursion is ~41% of TOTAL calls at 600 services (measured base ~1.39M + ~1624 calls/service), so as a fraction of the attackable machinery-only slice the 30-71% band is plausible, but the DELIVERED win is bounded below the 70% ceiling by the H7 value-merge floor that must still run per element. (2) Soundness of the shape-memo (the actual source of the win) holds ONLY because the dominant case is shape-invariant: under types.submodule (shorthandOnlyDefinesConfig=true, types.nix:1277) attrset elements provably CANNOT add options — verified: an element supplying an undeclared 'y' is rejected as unmatched. The engine memoizes the option SHAPE once on base and re-runs only the per-element value merge (with name bound). (3) The shape-divergence hazard is REAL but bounded: a FUNCTION-valued element (or shorthandOnlyDefinesConfig=false) routes through allModules' imports=[value] branch (types.nix:1377-1388) and CAN declare new options — verified: element b imports a module adding option y that element a lacks. The engine must gate on isFunction value and fall back to full re-collection for those elements; that gate is cheap (no deep eval), so the cache-overhead falsification does not net the prize negative. The mkOptionDefault default-prepend falsification also fails: _module.args.name already carries default(‹name›)+override(last loc) in the status quo, so it is not a NEW cost the engine introduces, and name-dependent DEFAULTS (serverName ? name) diverge VALUES not SHAPE.

D-axis scaling MEASURED (NIX_SHOW_STATS, nrFunctionCalls): synthetic attrsOf-submodule n=1→28679, n=250→632255, n=600→1480655, perfectly linear at 2424 calls/element; option-count sweep at n=250 opts={5,30,60}→{263505,632255,1074755} gives 59 calls/option/element — dominantly shape re-collection (mergeModules/zipAttrs transpose + unsafeGetAttrPos + fixupOptionType), since single-def leaf value-merge hits the ~free fast path (modules.nix:1214-1235). Real NixOS eval-config.nix systemd.services n=10→1410180, n=60→1491380 calls = ~1624 calls/service on a ~1.39M base. Shape-invariance under shorthand=true VERIFIED: /tmp/hc2_shorthand_soundness.nix rejects undeclared 'y' ('The option u.a.y does not exist'). Shape-DIVERGENCE via function element VERIFIED: /tmp/hc2_shape_divergence.nix element b imports a module declaring option y (default 99, set 7), absent on a — confirming the fallback gate is necessary. name-default soundness VERIFIED: /tmp/hc2_name_default.nix serverName defaults to element name without shape change. _module.check skippable slice MEASURED: n=250 check=true 639505 vs check=false 601505 calls (~6%, cost-center B). nixpkgs 2f4f625e9f48.

nixpkgs 2f4f625e9f48. extendModules re-enters the internal let-bound evalModules (modules.nix:386-393, :92); every submodule element routes through merge.v2/base.extendModules (types.nix:1445-1461); base computed once per type (:1393) -- so a wrapper on the engine entry callsite is bypassed on every extend (extendedPostmark=false). NIX_SHOW_STATS experiments: /tmp/hc2-fastpath.nix c10=34807 calls/104047 thunks, c100=111757/217807, c250=240007/407407 (~855 calls,~1264 thunks/element; //-copies 1.7568M->1.8384M, ~340/element, near the import floor). /tmp/hc2-split.nix heavy100(20 decls)=174055 vs light100(2 decls)=81655 -> 51.3 calls/decl/element collection, ~58% shareable at 20 decls x100. /tmp/hc2-floor.nix allset=144586 vs noneset=119386 (default-only still ~119K, fast path missed). /tmp/hc2-sound.nix svc-alpha/d-svc-alpha/OVERRIDE/d-OVERRIDE (per-element name/config values + mkForce correct under shared shape). adios reimplements its own foldl' merge (adios/types/types.nix:75,455,474,550) but keepsCompat=none. BENCHMARKS.md:30-76 composition axis (-26..-30% fn-calls) is a separate additive axis. Phase 1 report ~/Documents/papers/hola-architecture/analysis/phase-1-feasibility.md H2/H7/C9 -- C9's collect-vs-merge split is exactly what HC2 measures.
verdicts.nuance:
HC2 survives. (1) D-axis machinery scales with submodule-element density, separate from the //-storm (~855 calls,~1264 thunks/element; //-copies near the 1.756M import floor). (2) The shareable slice is re-collection (shape transpose): declsByName derives only from element-INDEPENDENT module.options declarations, while name/config-dependent values stay per-element thunks (verified byte-correct). ~51 calls/decl/element; a 20-decl submodule x100 elements is ~58% shareable, scaling into the claimed ~30-71% band as decl-density x element-count rises (40-decl systemd.services-class at ~600 = the 71% ceiling). (3) OWNS-extendModules is structural, not rhetorical: evalModules is a let-bound internal recursion that extendModules re-enters, routing every element through it; a wrapper on the entry callsite is bypassed on every extend (extendedPostmark=false). Owning it means BEING lib/modules.nix -- the expanded scope. The falsifier's two premises are TRUE but non-fatal and the claim anticipates them: the mkOptionDefault prepend pushes every defaulted option off the single-def fast path (default-only noneset still pays full merge), and the unshareable value-merge+base floor is real (~42% of heavy100) -- which is exactly WHY 70% is labeled a CEILING, not delivered. They cap the per-host D win WITHOUT netting it below the COMPOSITION-axis baseline, because those are different additive axes (Phase 1 H2 + BENCHMARKS composition-only workload). Genuine caveats, neither fatal: byte-compatible sharing requires per-element re-binding of name/config-dependent value thunks (sound but non-trivial); building a custom evalModules that hosts UNMODIFIED nixpkgs is far larger than adios's keepsCompat=none foldl' engine -- but the expanded scope licenses reimplementing the engine. Delta vs Phase 1 is real: orchestrate-around-verbatim ~0-per-host holds only under verbatim lib; a custom engine owning extendModules opens a second, additive, density-proportional per-host machinery win -- as a ceiling the prototype must net out, exactly as claimed.</nuance>
</invoke>

claim.claim: The merge algebra is NON-POSITIONAL and no gen primitive reproduces it, but a literature-grounded gen-merge CAN (Radul merge-lattice + Datafun monotone-semilattice-fixpoint + Leijen scoped-label def-stacks). RE-VERIFIED LIVE against nixpkgs: [normal 'n', mkForce 'c', mkDefault 'z', mkIf false, mkOrder 10] → ['c'] (force wins though neither first nor last; default+normal filtered by priority MIN-fold; mkIf-false evaporates); [mkAfter, normal, mkBefore] → ['first','mid','last']. foldLayers' three positional strategies (replace/append/recursive CSS-cascade) provably cannot model this. FALSIFIABLE: gen-merge must satisfy L1-L4 AND byte-match lib/types across the wrapper×type cross-product.
claim.whyLoadBearing: H7 is the irreducible kernel. Whether gen-merge can be byte-identical is the go/no-go for the whole 'reimplement-don't-orchestrate' thesis. verdicts.nuance: HC3 splits into two halves with opposite verdicts. UPHELD half: the PROPERTY ALGEBRA (dischargeProperties mkIf/mkMerge -> filterOverrides' priority MIN-fold -> sortProperties stable order-sort) is genuinely non-positional and IS byte-reproducible by a Radul/Leijen def-stack fold; foldLayers provably cannot model it. The claim's live L1/L2 results reproduce exactly (verified). REFUTED half (the load-bearing falsifiable clause): 'a self-contained gen-merge CAN byte-match lib/types across the wrapper x type cross-product.' This fails because the merge algebra is NOT a self-contained value-combinator — type.merge is mutually recursive with evalModules and several type.merges dispatch on runtime value-shape. A gen-merge byte-matching the cross-product would have to BE the custom evalModules, not a primitive it calls. This does not block hola's reimplement thesis; it dissolves the boundary the claim draws and forces the hybrid the brief named as fallback (own A/B/D + property pre-pass; HOST lib's type.merge/.merge.v2 verbatim at the leaf) to be the ONLY sound option, not a fallback. A narrowly-scoped HC3 ('gen-merge reproduces the property-discharge pre-pass, then hands filtered+sorted defs to a hosted type.merge') would be upholdable.
verdicts.evidence: Five counterexamples, all run live (nix-instantiate, nixpkgs=~/Documents/repos/nixpkgs). (1) type.merge is mutually recursive with evalModules, not a leaf combinator: submodule.merge.v2 calls evalModules (lib/types.nix:1393,1445-1449); attrsWith/anything-set re-enter mergeDefinitions per key (types.nix:1015,1340-1363); functionTo.merge defers into a __functor body (types.nix:1253-1264). (2) anything dispatches on builtins.typeOf of the FORCED value (types.nix:331-365): two distinct ints under types.anything THROW (a6 -> tryEval success=false), and {a=[1]}+{a=[2]} THROWS because lists under anything go to mergeEqualOption (equality) not concat — the SAME syntactic def merges differently by declared-vs-runtime type. Datafun's fixed-per-cell lattice cannot model a value-chosen lattice. (3) mergeDefaultOption int rule requires all-equal-or-throw even at equal priority (options.nix:439-442; a2 1+2 -> throw, a2b 5+5 -> 5); mergeEqualOption throws with showOption/showDefs/prioritySuggestion provenance strings (options.nix:509-517) that 'byte-match' must reproduce. (4) override and order are orthogonal axes and wrapper-NESTING-ORDER changes the filtered priority: dischargeProperties recurses only through merge/if, never order/override (modules.nix:1375-1385); getPrio reads only the outermost wrapper _type (modules.nix:1418). So mkOrder 1500 (mkForce ['z']) + mkForce ['a'] -> ['a'] (the z silently filtered at effective prio 100 vs the sibling's 50) — a partial, nesting-sensitive two-pass beyond L1-L4's flat fold. (5) priority composes through submodule recursion: mkForce {p=2;} on a submodule wipes sibling q='keep' back to its default (s1 -> {p=2;q='d'}), via whole-def filtering then per-option re-merge falling to mkOptionDefault. Also: filterOverrides' is min-fold over getPrio with strip (modules.nix:1415-1436); sortProperties is a stable sort by order priority (modules.nix:1449-1465). gen ships ZERO priority/override primitives today: gen/lib is a single 53-line mkGenLibs.nix; grep -rln 'mkForce|filterOverrides|highestPrio|mkOverride|priority' over gen/lib + gen/flakeModules returns empty — 'gen-merge' is entirely prospective. Experiment files: /tmp/hc3_l1.nix (L1/L2 reproduced ['c'] and ['first','mid','last']), /tmp/hc3_attack2.nix, /tmp/hc3_attack3.nix.

verdicts.nuance: HC3 is refuted as stated on three independent grounds, yet its go/no-go ENGINEERING conclusion survives inverted: the correct answer is "host lib.types merge VERBATIM," which is exactly the hybrid the claim's WHY-IT-MATTERS names as the fallback. (1) The headline "merge algebra is NON-POSITIONAL" is empirically FALSE: the priority/override sub-layer is non-positional (mkForce wins regardless of position) but the residual same-priority merge is strictly positional/non-commutative in module declaration order. The claim conflates the two; the real algebra is positional-by-default, priority-overridable. (2) The merge is NOT a (semi)lattice join — it is partial and THROWS on conflict (mergeEqualOption, mergeOneOption, two equal-priority mkForce), whereas Radul's lattice merge is total/commutative/associative with a contradiction VALUE; the den paper itself records option conflict as "error with diagnostic," not a join. So "Radul lattice CAN reproduce it" over-claims. (3) The claim re-verifies against the STALE legacy pipeline; live nixpkgs uses merge.v2 (per-element order/override peeling, escaping valueMeta, v2-coherence check) and submodule.merge.v2 is not a value-merge at all but a fresh RECURSIVE evalModules — so a byte-identical gen-merge must reproduce the whole evaluator recursively plus quirks (e.g. mkOrder(mkForce x) leaks the unstripped override into the value). foldLayers is confirmed purely positional and priority-blind, as conceded. CONDITIONS WHERE IT HOLDS: a closed sub-grammar (single-level options, no v2 types, no submodules, explicit Leijen def-stack ordering) — but that fails the claim's own bar of byte-matching the full wrapper×type cross-product. Net: gen-merge COULD match a restricted slice but CANNOT soundly+byte-match live lib/types; hola must call .merge/.merge.v2 verbatim — the Phase 1 orchestrate-don't-reimplement verdict applies to the merge kernel even under the expanded reimplement-the-engine scope.

verdicts.nuance:
HC3 is a TRUE NEGATIVE wrapped in an UNSUPPORTED POSITIVE plus a FALSE FRAMING; refuted on the load-bearing positive ("gen-merge CAN byte-match") and on the "NON-POSITIONAL" characterization, while its negative half ("no existing gen primitive reproduces it") and its two experiments stand.

WHAT HOLDS. The negative claim about existing primitives is correct: gen-algebra foldLayers (pure/rec.nix:200-205) is strictly positional — replace=foldl' last-wins, append=foldl' (++) list-order, recursive=foldl' (//); no priority integers, no MIN-fold, no mkIf discharge. It provably cannot model priority resolution. The two live experiments reproduce byte-exactly against ~/Documents/repos/nixpkgs/lib: r1=[n, mkForce c, mkDefault z, mkIf false, mkOrder 10]→"c" and r2=[mkAfter, normal, mkBefore]→["first","mid","last"]. The scalar priority pipeline (filterOverrides' MIN-fold modules.nix:1431; dischargeProperties mkIf-false→[] :1379-1385; sortProperties :1449-1465) is real and genuinely non-positional FOR PRIORITY SELECTION.

WHY REFUTED — three implementation-reality breaks.

(1) "NON-POSITIONAL" is false as a general statement. Verified live: types.listOf int, defs [1 2] then [3 4] → [3,4,1,2] (NOT [1,2,3,4]); types.lines "alpha" then "beta" → "beta\nalpha". Same-priority value merges are strongly POSITIONAL, ordered by module collection order (reverseList (doCollect{}).modules, modules.nix:275; consumed positionally by zipAttrs over configs :789-852). The claim conflates priority-resolution (non-positional MIN-fold) with value-merge (positional for list/lines/string/attrs). The r2 experiment only looks non-positional because mkOrder RE-SORTS defs; strip the wrappers and raw collection order governs.

(2) The go/no-go positive ("gen-merge CAN byte-match lib/types across the wrapper×type cross-product") is unproven and, for the hard cases, equivalent to "reimplement lib/modules.nix." type.merge is NOT a lattice join — it is a 28-way per-type dispatch (types.nix), 12 carrying merge.v2 (attrsOf, listOf, attrListWith, either, coercedTo, submoduleWith, addCheck...) that are MUTUALLY RECURSIVE with mergeDefinitions and evalModules. The submodule merge IS the evaluator: types.nix:1445-1461 merge.v2 = base.extendModules { modules = [{_module.args.name=last loc;}] ++ allModules defs; prefix = loc; } → configuration.config — a fresh recursive evalModules per element. attrsOf v2 (types.nix:1015), listOf v2 (:730), either v2 (:1659), coercedTo v2 (:1758) all call back into mergeDefinitions per child. Live deep test: attrsOf(submodule{port,tags}) with svc.web defined in two modules → fresh recursive evalModules merges mkForce 443 AND orders mkBefore[z] before [a]→["z","a"] AND injects the option default — priority + positional concat + defaults + submodule fixpoint interleaved in one recursion. To byte-match this gen-merge would have to reproduce: the v1/v2 dual path (modules.nix:1246-1306), checkV2MergeCoherence (:1161-1182), and thread valueMeta/headError/optionalValue/highestPrio/def.file (41 refs in modules.nix) — i.e. cost centers A (shape transpose), B (_module.check), and D (O(N×M) submodule recursion) ALL return. The cited literature (Radul merge-lattice / Datafun monotone-fixpoint / Leijen scoped-labels) models the SCALAR priority lattice; none models "merge = recurse into a fresh typed module fixpoint," which is exactly Phase 1's irreducible kernel (cost center D + H7).

(3) Even the L1-L4 falsifiable spec is mis-scoped. The priority pipeline (MIN-fold + sort + discharge) is a few-hundred-line job and is the EASY ~5%; but it runs in mergeDefinitions AROUND type.merge (modules.nix:1188-1254), not instead of it. Byte-matching the priority pre-merge does not byte-match the value merge; the cross-product the spec demands bottoms out at submodule/attrsOf/either where the merge IS the evaluator.

CONDITIONS. HC3 would hold if restricted to: scalar leaf options whose type.merge is mergeEqualOption/mergeOneOption/mergeDefaultOption AND single-or-priority-resolved defs — there gen-merge can own the pipeline and call a thin terminal join. It FAILS the moment a type is a v2/recursive/positional merge (list, lines, string concat, attrsOf, listOf, submodule, either, coercedTo, freeform), which is the bulk of real nixpkgs modules. Net: this vindicates the WHY-IT-MATTERS hybrid fallback — hola must host lib.types' .merge/.merge.v2 VERBATIM for typed/recursive cases and may own only the scalar priority pipeline. HC3's own go/no-go therefore resolves NO-GO for full reimplement-don't-orchestrate byte-match; GO only for the scalar pre-merge slice — not what the claim asserts.

verdicts.nuance: The claim's MECHANISM description is accurate and verified live — but its load-bearing assertions ("a from-scratch engine must implement BOTH or silently mis-merge every modern container" and the "addCheck is v2-universal" enumeration) are both false against source. REFUTED on three independent grounds. (1) The claim's own falsification criterion is met by its own example: `addCheck` is NOT uniformly v2. types.nix:1806-1835 dispatches `if elemType.merge ? v2` — over a scalar (`addCheck types.int`) it emits a legacy `check = x: elemType.check x && check x` with merge=plain-function; experiment confirms hasV2=false. So `addCheck`, listed by the claim among types that "must" be v2, refutes the universality. (Also scalar str/int/bool/path/attrs/raw/anything/nullOr/enum/uniq/lines/functionTo all hasV2=false.) (2) "Must implement BOTH" is false: every v2 type's legacy entry IS the v2 path — `merge.__functor = self: loc: defs: (self.v2 {loc;defs;}).value` (types.nix:718-720,790,1009,1447,1655,1746,1817). Experiment: legacy `merge loc defs` == v2 `.value` (equal=true) for listOf/attrsOf/either/coercedTo/nonEmptyListOf; and the full PRE-v2 caller (`all check; then merge loc defs`) runs correctly TODAY on listOf/attrsOf/either/coercedTo/submoduleWith (submodule default resolves), with `check` (now an attrset) still callable as a function via its `__functor` transparently. A custom engine can call ONLY the single `type.merge loc defs` interface and merge every modern container soundly; it need not dispatch on v2. (3) The priority/order/mkForce/mkOverride/mkIf algebra lives in mergeDefinitions (modules.nix:1188-1212), NOT in the v2 distinction — verified through real evalModules (mkForce→["forced"], mkOrder→sorted, mkOverride 50→wins, type error→throws). RESIDUAL GRAIN OF TRUTH (does not save the claim): legacy-only calling loses `valueMeta` capture — but it has exactly ONE production consumer, nixos/modules/security/apparmor.nix:131 (sub-option isDefined for an assertion), plus lib/tests; and the v2 headError yields a single consolidated TypeError vs legacy per-def showDefs text — different diagnostic string, identical throw/no-throw decision. Net: v2 adds metadata capture + an alternate-coded type-error message, NOT new merge semantics. Phase-1's H7 'type.merge' framing already captures the load-bearing semantics; HC4 surfaces a real recent mechanism but mis-frames it as a parity OBLIGATION. CONDITIONS WHERE A WEAKER VERSION HOLDS: an engine that wants byte-identical `options.*.valueMeta` (to host apparmor.nix unmodified and pass lib/tests/modules.sh valueMeta cases) must EITHER call lib's v2 merge OR reconstruct the sub-configuration handle itself — but that is the claim's own recommended hybrid, which is precisely why the duality stays lib-internal and invisible to the engine. Timeline note: v2 introduced 2025-08-13 (70ab11c2/5d72133a), coherence-check 2025-10-23 (93ea59f6), attrListOf 2026-05-24 (43d998e6) — window is wider than the claim's 'Aug-Oct 2025', reinforcing the 'actively evolving, don't pin it' caution, which again argues for calling lib not reimplementing.

verdicts.nuance: HC5 is half-true and materially overstated where it matters. The SEAM is real: lib.extend over the two attrs (lib.evalModules + lib.modules.evalModules) DOES intercept (a) the root eval and (b) the submodule-TYPE base; the lib-threading to real eval-config.nix is genuine and is even how nixpkgs' own nixosSystem works (flake.nix:64-68 passes lib=final into eval-config.nix:32 -> eval-config-minimal.nix:38 lib.evalModules). The 'overriding submoduleWith/attrsOf is a NO-OP' sub-claim is FACTUALLY CORRECT (probe4: internal attrsOf(submodule) never calls the overridden lib.types.submoduleWith; customSubmoduleUsed=false) — but that is evidence of a GAP, not of injection success. The claim FAILS on its load-bearing assertion: injection is NOT 'complete' and the sentinel-into-a-systemd.services-attrsOf-submodule story does not mean the custom engine evaluated the elements. Per-element submodule merge goes through base.extendModules (types.nix:1452,1470), and extendModules (modules.nix:386) calls the modules.nix-LOCAL let-bound evalModules — NOT lib.modules.evalModules — so the lib.extend overlay is structurally bypassed for every attrsOf/listOf element. The sentinel can still appear in element config only because the override-injected module rides along inside base's captured regularModules through the un-overridden internal recursion; the custom engine itself never runs per element. This is decisive because Phase-1 cost-center (D), the multiplicative O(NxM) per-element evaluations, is exactly the hot path a custom engine must OWN to save anything, and is exactly the path two-attr lib.extend cannot reach. To intercept (D) you must override extendModules (a per-eval-instance closure, not a lib attr) or fork modules.nix/types.nix — contradicting 'no fork / exactly two attrs / complete'. The dual-run parity harness over a full host's baseModules would, run honestly, expose this: root configDiff can be [] while the custom engine performed ~none of the per-service submodule work. claim.claim: H1 (//-storm), H6 (no cross-process persistence), the value-merge SEMANTICS, and import-closure forcing survive a full custom engine UNCHANGED. The //-storm is the package-set fixpoint (~1,756,268 copies before any output forced; submodule recursion contributes 0.017%/element). Reading one trivial leaf forces 601,273 fn-calls ≈ forcing attrNames config (601,201) — proving the transpose key-union is eager and read-independent; the union is hoistable across elements but NOT prunable (H3/H4). All runtime read-capture / reverse-edge override is INTRA-EVAL only (H6: lambdas non-serializable, eval-cache keys only locked-rev). FALSIFIABLE: any engine rewrite that moves the copy floor >1%, or any pure-Nix mechanism that persists a forced thunk across nix-eval invocations.
claim.whyLoadBearing: Bounds the upside honestly. The reframe attacks A/B/D (machinery) but cannot touch the copy floor or cross-process incrementality. hola must measure ALL wins NET of the fixed ~1.85M-copy / ~2.69M-fn-call base; cross-host incrementality REQUIRES an out-of-language store outside the pure-framework scope.

verdicts.nuance: HC6 survives a full custom-engine attack, but the expanded scope sharpens (not breaks) it: the "hoistable but NOT prunable" union is attackable in its CONSTANT (a custom lazy-per-key reader cut module-machinery fn-calls 8x, 368->45 fn-calls per unrelated module, and flattened copies to a constant 494) yet NOT in its LINEARITY (reading one leaf stays O(#modules-loaded) — finding the defs for one key still walks every module's config; sublinearity requires a pre-built reverse index that itself pays the full walk once, which IS the claim's "hoistable" amortization). True pruning is blocked at the SOUNDNESS level by H4: 274 dynamic `config.${...}` selects in nixos/modules (e.g. virtualisation/oci-containers.nix:419 `config.users.users.${effectiveUser}`, postgrey.nix:69 `getAttrFromPath p config`) make the reverse index statically undecidable, so a compat-exact engine must conservatively retain all modules that COULD define a read path. Numeric caveat: the "0.017%/element" submodule figure measures HIGH — I get 181 copies/element = 0.0103% of the 1.75M floor (577 fn-calls/element), so the claim is conservative there. Everything else reproduces to the digit. The expanded scope's A/B/D-are-machinery reframe is fully consistent with HC6: the 8x machinery win lives entirely above the copy floor and is exactly the slice Phase 1 already concedes is attackable.

verdicts.nuance: HC6 holds under the eval-semantics-soundness lens, with one boundary clarification that does not refute it but must be stated explicitly. The claim's load-bearing numbers all reproduce on nixpkgs 2f4f625e / Lix 2.96 within revision drift: bare import = 1,756,265 copies (cited 1,756,268); read-1-leaf = 601,258 fncalls (cited 601,273) vs attrNames-config = 601,195 (cited 601,201), diff 63 — proving the option-namespace transpose is eager and read-independent (cost-center A); submodule recursion = 0.0151%/element pure (cited 0.017%); lambda serialization throws (H6). The expanded-scope reframe attacks A/B/D as machinery, but it CANNOT touch the two things HC6 actually fences: (1) the import copy floor lives inside nixpkgs source (lib/customisation.nix, pkgs/stdenv/booter.nix extends `prev // overlay`, fixed-points.nix:333), so a custom engine hosting UNMODIFIED modules must `import nixpkgs {}` and cannot rewrite those `//` operators without forking nixpkgs — violating hola's defining constraint (self-enforcing floor); (2) H6 cross-process is a pure-language limit (no thunk serialization) unaffected by any in-language engine. THE ONE OVER-READING TO GUARD: 'the copy floor' in HC6 means the IMPORT sub-component (1.756M, intrinsic), NOT total config-eval copies. Module machinery adds +79,191 copies = 4.51% over the import floor when a real config is forced; a custom engine attacking cost-center A's copy fraction (applyModuleArgs // arg-synthesis modules.nix:724-739, zipAttrs transpose) could move TOTAL copies >1% — the expanded scope explicitly makes A attackable. So the falsifiable threshold 'moves the copy floor >1%' is sound only because copies and merge/fn-call cost are independent counters (verified: a 1000-key // chain copies 1004 slots with values unforced; nrOpUpdateValuesCopied is slot-copy-at-WHNF, orthogonal to merge). A careless conflation of 'the floor' with 'all copies' would be false; HC6 as written survives. Also note: forcing serviceConfig.ExecStart inflates per-element cost to 0.0725% via script-generation — HC6's 0.017% is correct only for pure submodule recursion, not for fully-forced units.

verdicts.nuance: HC6's four load-bearing pillars survive a full custom HOAG engine, but its READ-INDEPENDENCE ILLUSTRATION is over-stated and must be re-grounded — the over-statement does not break the conclusion, but a Phase-2 reader would mis-budget if they take the 601K/1.835M trivial-leaf figure as "intrinsic floor." THE REAL DECOMPOSITION I MEASURED: a custom engine that hosts the 2046 nixpkgs base modules verbatim is split cleanly into (intrinsic, immovable) the package-set fixpoint copy storm, vs (machinery, attackable) the _module.check shape-walk and the eager transpose fn-calls. (1) COPY FLOOR pillar — UPHELD on the real target. bare import<nixpkgs>{} forcing only attrNames = 1,756,265 nrOpUpdateValuesCopied (HC6 said 1,756,268, rev drift). On a REAL host build (system.build.toplevel.drvPath — the actual hola target), copies = 12,206,279 and disabling _module.check moves it only -0.2% (drvPath byte-identical 19231n2hz...), comfortably under HC6's own >1% falsification threshold. The custom engine cannot escape it because building toplevel forces the pkgs fixpoint regardless of merge implementation. (2) READ-INDEPENDENCE ILLUSTRATION — over-stated, must be qualified. HC6 says "reading one trivial leaf forces 601,273 fn-calls ~= attrNames config 601,201, proving the union eager and read-independent." I reproduced this EXACTLY (leaf 601,274; allnames 601,202; delta 72 calls — eager union confirmed). BUT that 601K/1.835M figure is ~60% fn-calls / 98% copies attributable to _module.check (cost-center B): with check=false the SAME trivial-leaf read drops to 240,140 fn-calls / 30,762 copies (-60%/-98%), and pkgs is no longer forced (a pkgs=throw survives the read). So the eager union HC6 points at is the CHECK shape-walk (machinery a custom engine removes), NOT the import-closure floor (intrinsic). HC6 conflates "the union is eager and read-independent" (true — it is, B forces it) with "this proves the intrinsic floor is read-independent" (false — the intrinsic floor is forced by BUILDING, not by READING a leaf; a check=false read of a pkgs-free option avoids it). The honest statement: the eager-union/read-independence property is real but is a property of the (removable) check+transpose machinery; the intrinsic copy floor is forced by materializing toplevel, not by reading. (3) SUBMODULE pillar — UPHELD. measured 265 copies/element (0.0151% of the 1.756M floor; HC6 said 0.017% — same order, off by rev). The real per-element submodule tax is fn-calls (1,626/element), not copies; either way the //-storm is NOT driven by submodule recursion. (4) NO CROSS-PROCESS PERSISTENCE pillar — UNCONDITIONALLY UPHELD, unrefutable. toJSON(lambda) throws "cannot convert a function to JSON"; toString(lambda) throws; (x:x)==(x:x) is structurally false (functions have no value-identity); the only serialization escape is the store/IFD, explicitly out of pure-framework scope. A forced option-tree fixpoint is a closure graph, not a serializable string. (5) VALUE-MERGE SEMANTICS pillar — UPHELD, inherits verified H7. grep confirms ZERO mkOverride/mkForce/filterOverrides/dischargeProperties anywhere in gen; foldLayers is documentation-only and priority-blind. HC6 carefully claims only merge SEMANTICS survive (not implementation) — correct: the reframe's "implementation is machinery" is honored, the conflict-site algebra has no gen replacement. NET VERDICT: the bounded-upside thesis is sound — a custom engine attacks A/B/D (and I proved check is real removable machinery worth -60% fn-calls / -98% copies ON LEAF READS), but on the real materialized-host target all wins are NET of the fixed package-set fixpoint floor, and cross-host incrementality requires an out-of-language store. HC6 must be EDITED to stop using the 601K trivial-leaf number as the intrinsic-floor proof; cite the toplevel.drvPath 12.2M / -0.2%-under-check figure instead, which is the genuine immovable floor on the workload hola actually ships.

claim.claim: NO existing project occupies hola's niche — reimplement evalModules's MACHINERY while preserving nixpkgs option/merge SEMANTICS and going faster. Prior art splits cleanly: REPLACE+drop-semantics (adios/korora — author: 'value merging makes sense for NixOS, but the cost is too high'; own mkOverride at adios/default.nix:417) OR keep-semantics+attack-a-different-layer (Tvix/Lix/parallel-eval evaluator layer; NixOS-a-la-carte PR #148456 lazy-skip claiming 3-4x but CLOSED, killed by the H3/H4 option-namespace coupling; lazyAttrsOf/freeform laziness surgeries). adios's mkOverride genericClosure dirty-propagate (default.nix:458-476) is the nearest in-Nix incremental analogue but is intra-eval and tied to its replacement engine. verdicts.lens: implementation-realism: hunted for a real counterexample (a project hosting unmodified nixpkgs modules with a faster REIMPLEMENTED merge kernel) in actual source — adios core, gen libs, Tvix/parallel-eval, PR #148456, and the 2026 entrant the brief omits (modular services) — and tested whether the brief's causal story ("killed by H3/H4 coupling") matches the historical record.
verdicts.nuance: UPHELD, with one causal correction and one new-entrant ruling that both SHARPEN rather than weaken the claim. The two prior-art buckets hold verbatim under source inspection. REPLACE+drop-semantics: adios/adios/default.nix:179 mergeOptionsUnchecked is plain `lhs // rhs //` recursion; ZERO of mkOverride/mkIf/mkOrder/mkForce/priority exist anywhere in adios (grep clean) — its `mkOverride` (default.nix:417, `root: prevEval: {...}`) is a module-tree incremental-RE-EVAL function, NOT nixpkgs value-priority; the genericClosure dirty-propagate (default.nix:458-469) + `removeAttrs prevEval.args diff` (475-476) is intra-eval and bound to its own engine; motivation.md:3 calls adios "a radically simple ALTERNATIVE to the NixOS module system." KEEP-semantics+different-layer: Tvix (Rust VM) and Determinate parallel-eval run lib.evalModules/modules.nix VERBATIM (parallel-eval blog: "minimally invasive to the current evaluator"; no merge-kernel touch). NixOS-a-la-carte PR #148456 is CLOSED (2022-01-27, mergedAt=null) and its mechanism per roberth is exactly the H3/H4 namespace coupling: "imports all modules as before, does not evaluate optional modules except their enabledBy, checks resulting OPTIONS (not config!) for enabler paths, repeat until fixed point." No counterexample exists: nothing hosts unmodified nixpkgs modules with a faster REIMPLEMENTED merge kernel. The niche is genuinely open. CORRECTION (load-bearing for the differentiator): the brief says #148456 was "killed by the H3/H4 coupling" — the thread shows otherwise. The fixpoint WORKED (~40% reduction, single iteration when deps declared); it stalled on refactoring SCALE + CI ("more progress is blocked on having better CI and unit tests"), because every module must hand-declare enabledBy/requires. So the coupling makes internal lazy-skip EXPENSIVE (per-module annotation), not IMPOSSIBLE. This refines hola's leverage: driving lazy selection EXTERNALLY via gen schema/HOAG edges is real precisely because it SUPPLIES the per-module dependency declarations #148456 needed by hand — from the gen plane — rather than eliminating the need for them. NEW ENTRANT the brief omits: NixOS modular services (RFC 163 / PR #372170, active June 2026). It does NOT occupy the niche — README-modular-services.md: "not a replacement for a NixOS module ... uncertainty not acceptable for widely used modules yet"; it is a new AUTHORING pattern (system.services submodule tree) on the SAME verbatim lib.evalModules merge kernel, requiring module rewrites. It reinforces the gap. CONDITIONS: claim holds for the precise scope "reimplement MACHINERY + preserve nixpkgs option/merge SEMANTICS + host UNMODIFIED + faster"; it would FAIL only if a future project lands a drop-in evalModules with mkOverride/mkIf/mkOrder/type.merge fidelity hosting nixpkgs unmodified — none exists as of 2026-06.
